name: Sierra de Alcarama Loop
name_original: Sierra de Alcarama, Collado de la Mesta y Sárnago – circular desde Las Fuesas
region: Tierras Altas Y Valle Del Tera
description: Hike the moderate 9.9-mile Sierra de Alcarama, Collado de la Mesta y Sárnago circular route from Las Fuesas, exploring mountainous terrain a
region_intro: Waterfall hiking trails around Tierras Altas Y Valle Del Tera are characterized by elevated terrain, river valleys, and significant watercourses that create numerous cascades. The region features a landscape of hills and valleys, often carved by rivers, leading to a variety of waterfall formations. Hikers can expect to navigate paths through varied vegetation, from open areas to more sheltered sections alongside streams. This environment provides a natural setting for exploring diverse waterfall trails. Best waterfall hiking trails around Tierras Altas Y Valle Del Tera The most popular waterfall hiking route is Barranco del Val Trail, a 8.1 miles (13.1 km) trail that takes 3 hours 36 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores a notable ravine with flowing water features. Another top favourite among local hikers is Cascada del Pozo de las Truchas y Peña del Tío Fresquito – circular desde Ágreda (PR-SO 20 y 20.1 Senda Barranco del Val), a moderate 7.9 miles (12.7 km) path. This trail features a waterfall and rock formations, offering diverse scenery. Local hikers also love the Pico Frentes and La Toba Waterfall Loop Trail, a 5.9 miles (9.5 km) trail leading through a landscape with a peak and a prominent waterfall, often completed in about 2 hours 47 minutes. Hiking around Tierras Altas Y Valle Del Tera is defined by elevated terrain, river valleys, and numerous waterfalls. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult trails, catering to various ability levels. The routes in Tierras Altas Y Valle Del Tera are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 150 reviews. More than 1500 hikers have used komoot to explore Tierras Altas Y Valle Del Tera's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many waterfall hiking trails are available in Tierras Altas Y Valle Del Tera?
A: Tierras Altas Y Valle Del Tera offers a selection of 10 dedicated waterfall hiking trails. These routes vary in difficulty, providing options for different experience levels.

Q: Are there any easy waterfall hikes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, the region has 3 easy waterfall hikes. A great option for a more relaxed experience is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38162153" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente Ra – Cascada Mayor de Puente Ra loop from Lomos de Orios&lt;/a&gt;, which is 3.2 miles (5.1 km) long.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on waterfall hikes in Tierras Altas Y Valle Del Tera?
A: The terrain typically features elevated landscapes, river valleys, and significant watercourses that form numerous cascades. You'll navigate paths through varied vegetation, from open areas to more sheltered sections alongside streams, offering a diverse natural setting.

Q: Are there any circular waterfall walks in the area?
A: Yes, several waterfall trails are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1058987989" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Frentes and La Toba Waterfall Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a 5.9 miles (9.5 km) route that takes you through a landscape with a prominent waterfall and a peak.

Q: What do other hikers say about the waterfall trails in Tierras Altas Y Valle Del Tera?
A: The waterfall trails in Tierras Altas Y Valle Del Tera are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 150 reviews. Hikers often praise the natural beauty of the cascades and the varied landscapes.

Q: Are there any notable landmarks or attractions near the waterfall hiking trails?
A: Yes, the region is rich in history and natural beauty. You can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2718792" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Yanguas Historic Village&lt;/a&gt;, visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2718376" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of la Nava del Marqués de Magaña&lt;/a&gt;, or see the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3370754" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cow Sculptures of Sotillo del Rincón&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2089062" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cebollera Lagoon&lt;/a&gt; also offers a beautiful natural sight.

Q: What is the best time of year to visit Tierras Altas Y Valle Del Tera for waterfall hikes?
A: The best time to experience the waterfalls is typically during spring (April-June) when snowmelt and spring rains ensure the cascades are at their most powerful. Autumn (September-October) also offers beautiful scenery with changing foliage, though water levels might be lower.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging waterfall hikes for experienced hikers?
A: For those seeking a more challenging experience, there are 2 difficult trails in the region. While specific difficult waterfall routes are not detailed, the overall network provides options for advanced hikers looking for longer distances and greater elevation changes.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the waterfall trails?
A: Many trails in Tierras Altas Y Valle Del Tera are dog-friendly, but it's always recommended to check specific route details and local regulations before heading out. Ensure your dog is on a leash and that you carry waste bags to maintain the natural environment.

Q: What should I wear for waterfall hiking in this region?
A: Given the varied terrain and potential for wet conditions near waterfalls, waterproof hiking boots with good grip are essential. Layers of clothing are advisable, as temperatures can change, and a waterproof jacket will be useful for spray near cascades or unexpected rain.

Q: Are there any hidden gem waterfall hikes that are less crowded?
A: While specific 'hidden gems' are not highlighted, exploring some of the less-trafficked moderate routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20150602" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña del Tío Fresquito loop from Ágreda&lt;/a&gt;, might offer a more secluded experience compared to the most popular trails.

Q: Is public transport available to reach the waterfall hiking trailheads?
A: Public transport options in Tierras Altas Y Valle Del Tera can be limited, especially to more remote trailheads. It is generally advisable to plan for personal vehicle use or check local bus schedules to specific villages if you intend to rely on public transport.
tips: We are walking through the Sierra de Alcarama, located in the Collado de la Mesta and with an excellent panoramic view of the nature that surrounds us.
Beautiful and well-kept refuge that we find in the Collado de la Mesta, with excellent views of the steep climb to the top of the Sierra de Alcarama.
Nice views of the town of Sárnago while we enjoy hiking on a wide and quiet dirt road.

faqs.question: How long does the circular hike from Las Fuesas typically take?
faqs.answer: This moderate circular hike, covering approximately 15.9 km, typically takes around 4 hours and 42 minutes to complete. This duration is an estimate and can vary based on your pace and stops along the way.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this hike?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as moderate. While it involves a significant elevation gain of about 476 meters, the overall distance and terrain make it suitable for hikers with a reasonable level of fitness. Expect some ascents and descents, particularly around the Collado de la Mesta.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail begins and ends in Las Fuesas, as it is a circular route starting from this point. While specific parking details for Las Fuesas are not provided, it is generally advisable to look for designated parking areas or safe roadside spots in small villages like Las Fuesas when embarking on a hike in this region.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this hike?
faqs.answer: You can expect a varied terrain typical of the Sierra de Alcarama. This includes mountainous slopes, rocky sections, and paths winding through Mediterranean scrubland with rosemary, thyme, gorse, juniper, and holm oaks. Some areas may feature pine repopulations, and you might encounter rocky outcrops and the occasional glimpse of historical mining elements.

faqs.question: What wildlife and flora might I encounter?
faqs.answer: The Sierra de Alcarama is a Special Protection Area for Natural Values, making it rich in biodiversity. You have a good chance of spotting various raptors, including griffon vultures, golden eagles, Bonelli's eagles, and peregrine falcons, especially in rocky areas. The flora is predominantly Mediterranean, with aromatic plants like rosemary and thyme, alongside juniper and holm oaks.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: Key highlights on this route include the Collado de la Mesta, Sierra de Alcarama, which is a mountain pass offering scenic views. You'll also pass by the Collado de la Mesta Refuge, a hut that can provide a brief stop. Another notable point is the View of Sárnago, offering picturesque vistas of the village and surrounding landscape.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The best times to hike in the Sierra de Alcarama region are typically spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October). During these seasons, the temperatures are milder, and the landscape is often at its most vibrant, with blooming wildflowers in spring or rich autumnal colors. Summer can be very hot and dry, while winter may bring colder temperatures and potential snow at higher elevations.

faqs.question: Is this trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While specific regulations for this exact trail are not detailed, hiking with dogs is generally permitted in many parts of the Sierra de Alcarama. However, it is crucial to keep your dog on a leash, especially due to the presence of wildlife (like raptors and other protected species) and potential livestock. Always ensure your dog is well-behaved and that you clean up after them.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for hiking in Sierra de Alcarama?
faqs.answer: No, there are generally no specific permits or entrance fees required for hiking this trail or in the broader Sierra de Alcarama Natural Park. However, as it is a protected area, visitors are expected to respect the natural environment, stay on marked paths, and follow Leave No Trace principles.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities or water sources along the trail?
faqs.answer: The Collado de la Mesta Refuge is noted as a hut along the route, which might offer basic shelter, but it's not guaranteed to have amenities like water or food services. It is highly recommended to carry sufficient water and snacks for the entire duration of the hike, as natural water sources may be unreliable or unsafe to drink without purification.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this hike?
faqs.answer: For this moderate hike, you should bring sturdy hiking boots, sufficient water (especially in warmer months), snacks, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and appropriate clothing layers for changing weather conditions. A map or GPS device (like the komoot app) is also recommended for navigation, along with a first-aid kit.
